AI Harness
AI Sense
All Patterns
Depth: Thinnest Model role: Black box under test

Evaluation

Feeds benchmarks, measures outputs, and scores AI performance systematically

Real World Analogy

Like a food inspector visiting a restaurant. The inspector does not cook anything. They order dishes, taste them, score them against standards, and publish a rating. The restaurant (model) is unchanged by the inspection, but the results determine whether it stays open.

How It Works

Evaluation harnesses are the measurement tools of the AI world. They feed standardized test questions to AI models, score the answers, and report how well the model performs. The AI is treated as a black box: the harness sends in questions and measures what comes out without modifying anything. This is the thinnest wrapping of any harness type.

These systems range from academic benchmark suites that test general knowledge and reasoning to production monitoring tools that continuously check AI quality on real user traffic. Scoring can be as simple as checking if the answer matches exactly, or as sophisticated as using another AI to judge the quality of open ended responses.

Evaluation harnesses increasingly integrate with software development pipelines. Just as code changes must pass tests before deployment, AI system changes must pass evaluation benchmarks. This prevents deploying updates that accidentally make the AI worse.

Components

  • Standardized benchmark datasets with expected answers
  • Scoring functions from exact match to AI as judge evaluation
  • Comparison frameworks for testing model A versus model B
  • Red teaming and adversarial security scanning
  • CI/CD integration to block deployments that fail quality checks
  • Production monitoring for detecting quality drift on live traffic

Real World Examples

EleutherAI lm-evaluation-harness

Most widely used benchmark suite with 60+ tests, powers the Open LLM Leaderboard

Stanford HELM

Holistic evaluation scoring safety, fairness, accuracy, and robustness in a single run

Promptfoo

Open source CLI with built in red teaming and security scanning alongside evaluation

Braintrust

SaaS platform combining evaluation scoring with production tracing and CI enforcement

Key Terms

Benchmark

A standardized set of test questions used to measure and compare AI model performance.

LLM as judge

Using one AI model to evaluate the quality of another AI model's responses.

Red teaming

Deliberately trying to make an AI system fail through adversarial testing to discover vulnerabilities.

Related Patterns

  • Fine Tuning
  • Safety and Guardrail
